D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Ligand Field — Strong vs Weak, High vs Low Spin

when Δ₀ exceeds the pairing energy P, electrons prefer to pair up in t₂g (low spin) — Δ₀ < P keeps them spread across all five orbitals (high spin)

The ligand-field gap Δ₀ competes with the electron pairing energy P. Strong-field ligands (CN⁻, CO, NH₃) give large Δ₀ — electrons pair up first in t₂g (low-spin, fewer unpaired electrons). Weak-field ligands (I⁻, Br⁻, Cl⁻, H₂O, F⁻) give small Δ₀ — Hund's rule wins, electrons spread across all five orbitals (high-spin).

Drag the ligand from weak (left) to strong (right) along the spectrochemical series. Watch the electron distribution in the d⁶ Co(III) example flip — high-spin (4 unpaired) becomes low-spin (0 unpaired). Magnetic moment μ ≈ √(n(n+2)) Bohr magnetons tracks the change.
